idea: Baseline 7-kernel fused MoE pipeline from tutorial (00_baseline.co)
Fuse scatter into WGMMA, count+build, quant+sort+gather to reduce 7 kernels to 4
Apply all tutorial optimizations: parallel.async, grid swap, L2 persistence, QSG pipelining, Hillis-Steele scan, CUDA Graphs. DMA falls back to scalar copies (alignment issue).
Replace opt_copy (scalar 32-bit loads) with cp.async tiled_copy using SM80_CP_ASYNC_CACHEALWAYS<uint128_t> for LHS DMA. 128-bit coalesced loads via cp.async pipeline eliminate 60% L1TEX stalls.
Add __restrict__ to all kernel pointer params and --use_fast_math. +1.3% vs iter004.
Remove --maxrregcount=224 to allow more registers. Slightly worse, register cap helps occupancy.
Double-buffer K-loop pipelining (sA+sB x2). Regressed 7.2% due to doubled shared memory (49KB) reducing occupancy.
Added #pragma unroll to warp loops, fragment init, and scatter loops. No effect — compiler already unrolling at -O3.
Reduced maxrregcount from 224 to 128 for higher occupancy. Severe regression (-14%) from register spilling outweighing occupancy gains.
Changed cp.async from CACHEALWAYS to CACHEGLOBAL for LHS loads — bypass L1 for streaming data, frees L1 for scatter atomicAdd. +0.4% gain.
Changed L2 persistence from LHS to scatter output. Regressed 10% — LHS benefits more from L2 pinning due to cross-expert reuse.
Preloaded scale_b into registers + CACHEGLOBAL. Severe regression (-24%) from register spilling due to 16 extra floats per thread.
Added -Xptxas -dlcm=cg for all loads. No change — CACHEGLOBAL already explicit on LHS.

Double-buffer only RHS TMA sB 16KB keeping LHS single-buffered. Total shared 40KB. TMA prefetch next K-tile before WGMMA. Regressed 3.4% due to C7520 WGMMA serialization from divergent TMA barriers and increased shared memory reducing occupancy.
Added __launch_bounds__(128, 2) to all 3 WGMMA kernels. No measurable improvement - compiler already optimizing register usage well at 168 regs.
